canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, P-MMEVAL is introduced as a large-scale and comprehensive benchmark for the evaluation of LLMs. It is defined as a "parallel multilingual multitask benchmark" designed for consistent evaluation across 10 languages. The instrument is stated to cover both "fundamental NLP and capability-specialized tasks." The single source paper specifies that P-MMEVAL is built upon eight existing benchmarks and incorporates seven multilingual tasks. As there are no relationships provided in the data, its application in measuring specific constructs or behaviors is not detailed, though its stated purpose is the consistent evaluation of language models.
papers: CAVE: Detecting and Explaining Commonsense Anomalies in Visual Environments
Co-EvolvingLLMs and Embedding Models via Density-Guided Preference Optimization for Text Clustering

definitions.text: A parallel multilingual multitask benchmark covering fundamental NLP and capability-specialized tasks across 10 languages, designed for consistent evaluation of LLMs.
definitions.paper: Co-EvolvingLLMs and Embedding Models via Density-Guided Preference Optimization for Text Clustering
